ddls:
CREATE TABLE "employees" (
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each employee */,
  "satisfaction_level" REAL /* Satisfaction level of the employee (on a scale of 0 to 1) */,
  "last_evaluation" REAL /* Last performance evaluation score of the employee (on a scale of 0 to 1) */,
  "number_project" INTEGER /* Number of projects the employee is currently working on */,
  "average_monthly_hours" INTEGER /* Average monthly working hours of the employee */,
  "time_spend_company" INTEGER /* Time spent by the employee in the company (in years) */,
  "work_accident" INTEGER /* Number of work accidents the employee has been involved in */,
  "left" INTEGER /* Whether the employee has left the company (1 for yes, 0 for no) */,
  "promotion_last_5years" INTEGER /* Whether the employee has been promoted in the last 5 years (1 for yes, 0 for no) */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the department the employee belongs to */,
  "salary_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the salary level of the employee */,
  "job_role_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the job role of the employee */,
  "employee_name" TEXT /* Full name of the employee */,
  "employee_email" TEXT /* Email address of the employee */,
  "employee_phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the employee */,
  "employee_address" TEXT /* Address of the employee */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the employee */,
  "date_of_joining" TEXT /* Date when the employee joined the company */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the employee */,
  "marital_status" TEXT /* Marital status of the employee */,
  "education_level" TEXT /* Highest level of education completed by the employee */,
  "manager_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee's manager */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("employee_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employees_salary_id FOREIGN KEY ("salary_id") REFERENCES salaries ("salary_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employees_manager_id FOREIGN KEY ("manager_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employees_department_id FOREIGN KEY ("department_id") REFERENCES departments ("department_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employees_job_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("job_role_id") REFERENCES job_roles ("job_role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "departments" (
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each department */,
  "department_name" TEXT /* Name of the department */,
  "department_head_id" INTEGER /* ID of the department head */,
  "department_location" TEXT /* Location of the department */,
  "department_budget" INTEGER /* Annual budget allocated to the department */,
  "department_description" TEXT /* Description of the department's responsibilities and goals */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("department_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_departments_department_head_id FOREIGN KEY ("department_head_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "salaries" (
  "salary_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each salary level */,
  "salary_level" TEXT /* Level of the salary (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "salary_amount" INTEGER /* Actual salary amount in the local currency */,
  "currency" TEXT /* Currency in which the salary is paid */,
  "salary_increment" REAL /* Annual salary increment percentage */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("salary_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "job_roles" (
  "job_role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each job role */,
  "job_role_name" TEXT /* Name of the job role */,
  "job_role_description" TEXT /* Description of the job role's responsibilities and requirements */,
  "job_role_level" TEXT /* Level of the job role (e.g., junior, senior, manager) */,
  "job_role_min_salary" INTEGER /* Minimum salary for the job role */,
  "job_role_max_salary" INTEGER /* Maximum salary for the job role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("job_role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_evaluations" (
  "evaluation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance evaluation */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee being evaluated */,
  "evaluation_score" REAL /* Score of the performance evaluation (on a scale of 0 to 1) */,
  "evaluation_date" TEXT /* Date of the performance evaluation */,
  "evaluator_id" INTEGER /* ID of the evaluator */,
  "evaluation_comments" TEXT /* Comments from the evaluator */,
  "evaluation_type" TEXT /* Type of evaluation (e.g., annual, mid-year) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("evaluation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_evaluations_evaluator_id FOREIGN KEY ("evaluator_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_evaluations_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "projects" (
  "project_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each project */,
  "project_name" TEXT /* Name of the project */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the project */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the project */,
  "project_manager_id" INTEGER /* ID of the project manager */,
  "project_budget" INTEGER /* Budget allocated to the project */,
  "project_status" TEXT /* Current status of the project (e.g., ongoing, completed, on hold) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("project_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_projects_project_manager_id FOREIGN KEY ("project_manager_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "employee_projects" (
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee */,
  "project_id" INTEGER /* ID of the project */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the employee in the project */,
  "hours_worked" INTEGER /* Total hours worked by the employee on the project */,
  "project_role_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the employee's role in the project */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("employee_id", "project_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "work_accidents" (
  "accident_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each work accident */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee involved in the accident */,
  "accident_date" TEXT /* Date of the accident */,
  "accident_description" TEXT /* Description of the accident */,
  "accident_severity" TEXT /* Severity of the accident (e.g., minor, major) */,
  "accident_cost" INTEGER /* Cost incurred due to the accident */,
  "accident_location" TEXT /* Location where the accident occurred */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("accident_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_work_accidents_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "promotions" (
  "promotion_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each promotion */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee being promoted */,
  "promotion_date" TEXT /* Date of the promotion */,
  "new_job_role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the new job role */,
  "previous_job_role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the previous job role */,
  "promotion_reason" TEXT /* Reason for the promotion */,
  "promotion_effective_date" TEXT /* Date when the promotion becomes effective */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("promotion_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_promotions_new_job_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("new_job_role_id") REFERENCES job_roles ("job_role_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_promotions_previous_job_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("previous_job_role_id") REFERENCES job_roles ("job_role_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_promotions_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)
